outcome_intro: Run the Kaseya BMS service desk, contracts, and billing from your terminal - or let your AI agent do it. Ask in plain English which queues are underwater, which tickets are going stale, who's overloaded, how much of each contract you've burned, and what billable time is sitting unbilled - and get the answer instantly from a local mirror, without exporting a single report.

instead_of_say.instead: Export the morning ticket list to Excel and pivot it by queue to find where the backlog is building.
instead_of_say.say: Which queues are underwater this morning, and how many tickets are going stale?

instead_of_say.instead: Run the unbilled-time report, export it, and reconcile approved billable hours per client by hand before invoicing.
instead_of_say.say: How many approved billable hours are sitting unbilled per client right now?

instead_of_say.instead: Click through each technician's board to work out who has bandwidth for the next escalation.
instead_of_say.say: Who's overloaded and who can take the next ticket?

pain_points: MSPs on r/msp and MSPGeek repeatedly call BMS reporting the weak spot: the questions you ask every morning - queue backlog, aging tickets, who's overloaded - mean exporting grids to Excel and pivoting them by hand.

Month-end billing is the other recurring complaint: pulling approved-but-unbilled time per client out of BMS for the invoice run is a manual export-and-reconcile chore, and the API's 1,500-requests-per-hour-per-endpoint limit punishes any tool that asks the question live every time.

outcomes.question: Which queues are underwater and what's going stale before standup?
Which open tickets haven't been touched in a week, oldest first?
Who's overloaded and who can take the next ticket?
How much of each contract have we burned this quarter?
What approved billable time is ready to invoice, by account?
What's the open sales pipeline by stage, and which deals have slipped?
Find every ticket mentioning a phrase across the whole tenant
Sync the tenant into a local mirror for instant offline queries

different_vs_wrappers: Most Kaseya BMS integrations proxy each question into a live API call against the 1,500-per-hour-per-endpoint limit - fine for one record, but it falls over the moment you ask a fleet-wide or month-end question. This skill syncs the tenant into a local SQLite mirror with full-text search, so aggregate questions - queue health, contract burn, unbilled hours, weighted pipeline - are answered by one local join: instant, offline, and the agent sees the answer, not the raw data.
different_vs_vendor: The BMS console and its reports are built for clicking through one screen at a time; this skill answers the cross-entity questions the grid won't compute - per-contract burn, unbilled hours by account, weighted pipeline with slipped-deal flags - from your terminal or your AI agent, and complements the portal rather than replacing it.

faqs.a: Yes, on paid ChatGPT plans. ChatGPT connects to remote MCP servers over HTTPS, so you expose the local Kaseya BMS MCP server via a secure bridge. Step-by-step in the install guide.

faqs.q: Do I need to know how to code?
faqs.a: No. Paste one sentence into Claude Code or Codex and your agent does the install, or run a one-line installer. You enter your credentials once.

faqs.q: Is my Kaseya BMS data safe?
faqs.a: Your data stays on your machine. The CLI, MCP server, and the local mirror are all local. The AI sees query results, not raw bulk data, and credentials are never bundled or transmitted by MSP Skills.
Free. Apache-2.0 licensed. You pay only for whichever AI agent you already use.

faqs.q: Will this hit my Kaseya BMS API rate limits?
faqs.a: Rarely. BMS allows 1,500 requests per hour per endpoint. Reads default to the local SQLite mirror (--data-source auto), so day-to-day questions cost zero API calls; you only spend the budget when you sync or query live.

faqs.q: Do I need to be a Kaseya customer, and how do I authenticate?
faqs.a: Yes - you need a Kaseya BMS tenant and an API user. The skill authenticates as that user with 'kaseya-bms-cli auth login' (your BMS username, password, and tenant name), and can only do what that user is permitted to do. You can also paste a pre-minted JWT via KASEYA_BMS_TOKEN.

faqs.q: Will this replace my Kaseya BMS portal?
faqs.a: No. It's a faster path for the questions you ask every day - queue health, unbilled time, pipeline - and for letting an AI agent drive the service desk. The BMS portal stays your system of record.

governance_summary: The skill reads everything - tickets, CRM, contracts, finance, projects - and can create and update records across the service desk, CRM, finance, and projects, so keep an autonomous agent to reads plus previewed (--dry-run) writes with a human approving each write. Deletes, the back-office 'admin' group, and any command that returns stored credentials (ITGlue or VSA access info) are human-in-the-loop only. The strongest control is the scope of the BMS API user you authenticate as.

safety_tiers.examples: All 243 read commands plus the analytics rollups: queue-health, stale-tickets, unbilled, pipeline, servicedesk get-ticket, search
124 create/update commands across the service desk, CRM, finance, and projects: servicedesk post-ticket, servicedesk assign-ticket, crm post-account, finance mark-invoices-as-sent, plus import
safety_tiers.policy: Preview with --dry-run, then a reviewed write

safety_tiers.examples: Commands that return or store secrets: integrations get-itgpassword-value, integrations get-vsa-access-info, security refresh-token, auth login
21 delete commands: servicedesk delete-ticket, crm delete-contact, system delete-attachment
The 75-command admin group: webhooks, workflows, services, K1 access control, Teams channels
